artifacts.title: PRD: IdeaForge
artifacts.markdown:
# PRD: IdeaForge

## Goals
- Turn voice notes into reviewed product artifacts.
- Keep the primary path local-first until the user enables backend AI.

## Requirements
- Preserve transcript, question, artifact, and workflow provenance.
- Keep workflow output reviewable before build handoff.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Generated artifacts include acceptance checks before a workflow is accepted.
- Required schema fields are present in the PRD artifact.

## Validation
- Evidence comes from the captured IdeaProject assumptions and validation experiments.

## Risks
- Provider output can drift unless schema and rubric checks stay enforced.

## Next Steps
- Review blockers and run the production verifier before handoff.

artifacts.title: Idea Brief: IdeaForge
artifacts.markdown:
# Idea Brief: IdeaForge

## Summary
IdeaForge turns rough voice notes from Watch, iPhone, or Mac into reviewed planning artifacts that a builder can inspect before implementation.

## Target User
Solo builders and product operators who capture ideas away from the desk and need a structured first pass before opening an editor.

## Problem
Voice ideas lose context when they become raw transcripts. The product must preserve the core problem, source evidence, and unresolved blocking questions before any build handoff.

## Next Questions
- Who is the first user who needs this badly enough to pay?
- Which privacy mode should be visible before cloud AI is enabled?
- What evidence proves a Codex-ready packet saves implementation prep time?

## Risks
Provider output can drift unless every generated artifact remains schema-checked and reviewable.

artifacts.title: MVP Roadmap: IdeaForge
artifacts.markdown:
# MVP Roadmap: IdeaForge

## Scope
Build the smallest local-first loop: capture an idea, preserve transcript context, generate reviewed idea brief, roadmap, and validation plan artifacts, and keep export gated behind user review.

## Sequence
1. Verify capture and transcript persistence on local sample projects.
2. Generate schema-backed planning artifacts for the selected IdeaProject.
3. Review blockers, risks, and validation evidence before export.
4. Run swift test, the production verifier, and the macOS launch smoke before handoff.

## Risks
- Cloud AI must stay behind explicit backend and privacy-mode gates.
- Watch-to-iPhone transfer and background upload still need physical-device proof.
- Release claims remain blocked until signing and App Store assets exist.

## Next Steps
- Add provider prompt fixtures for each default planning workflow.
- Exercise the running Mac and iOS app surfaces after each workflow change.
- Keep the gap map honest about external blockers.

artifacts.title: Validation Plan: IdeaForge
artifacts.markdown:
# Validation Plan: IdeaForge

## Assumptions
- Builders will trust a local-first planning app if cloud AI is explicit and reviewable.
- Codex packets are more useful than raw transcript exports when they include acceptance checks.
- Watch capture is valuable only if transfer and retry behavior survive real device conditions.

## Experiments
- Interview builders who capture ideas while away from their Mac.
- Compare manual PRD drafting time against the generated planning packet.
- Run a privacy review that checks logs, exports, and sync conflicts for raw transcript or token leakage.

## Success Metrics
- At least 7 of 10 builders say the reviewed packet saves 30 or more minutes.
- Zero provider outputs are accepted when required schema fields are missing.
- Production verifier, app launch smoke, and privacy-safe log review pass for the slice.

## Risks
- Physical-device behavior, deployed backend orchestration, and App Store signing remain release blockers until proven with direct evidence.

artifacts.title: Codex Packet: IdeaForge
artifacts.markdown:
# Codex Packet: IdeaForge

## Repo Context
Implement inside the IdeaForge repository using the existing SwiftPM, XcodeGen, SwiftUI, and IdeaForgeCore service patterns.

## Tasks
- Inspect current source and docs before editing.
- Make the smallest production slice that satisfies the reviewed workflow.
- Preserve privacy gates and avoid external writes without explicit approval.

## Checks
- Run swift test.
- Run ./script/verify_production.sh.
- Run ./script/build_and_run.sh --verify.

## Validation
- Evidence comes from the current IdeaProject, workflow history, and release checklist.

## Risks
- Codex execution can mutate local or remote state if approval boundaries are unclear.

## Approval Boundary
Review before running external tools or making public writes without operator approval.

artifacts.title: Architecture: IdeaForge
artifacts.markdown:
# Architecture: IdeaForge

## Decision
Keep app behavior in shared IdeaForgeCore services and keep backend AI behind explicit privacy mode and Keychain-backed configuration.

## Components
- macOS SwiftUI planning studio.
- iOS bridge and upload queue.
- watchOS capture shell.
- Backend AI adapter with output contract validation.

## Risks
- Provider structured output can drift unless prompt fixtures and runtime contract validation remain enforced.
- Release claims are blocked until signing, device, and accessibility evidence exists.

## Validation
- Verify through Swift tests, mock backend self-test, production verifier, and app launch smoke.

## Next Steps
- Expand live provider structured-output enforcement and prompt regression coverage before enabling real AI broadly.

artifacts.title: Idea Brief: IdeaForge
artifacts.markdown:
# Idea Brief: IdeaForge

## Summary
IdeaForge captures raw ideas from Watch, iPhone, or Mac and turns them into reviewed build packets.

## Target User
Solo builders and product operators who need a structured handoff before implementation.

## Problem
Voice ideas lose context when they become raw transcript files without reviewable product structure.

## Next Questions
- Who is the first paying user?
- Which backend AI gates must be visible before cloud processing?
- What proof makes the packet ready for build?

## Risks
- Provider output can drift without schema checks.

## Validation
- Review against workflow history, assumptions, and production verifier output.

artifacts.title: PRD: IdeaForge
artifacts.markdown:
# PRD: IdeaForge

## Goals
- Preserve capture context from idea to build packet.
- Keep backend AI explicit, gated, and reviewable.

## Requirements
- Store transcript, questions, artifacts, workflow runs, and export state with provenance.
- Block unreviewed Codex or cloud actions by default.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Full build packet artifacts validate against required schema fields.
- Export requires review status and documented checks.

## Risks
- Physical-device and deployed-backend behavior remain release blockers.

## Next Steps
- Run production verification and update the readiness label after each slice.

artifacts.title: Architecture: IdeaForge
artifacts.markdown:
# Architecture: IdeaForge

## Decision
Keep product behavior in shared IdeaForgeCore services, with SwiftUI targets consuming the same privacy-safe workflow and sync contracts.

## Components
- macOS planning studio.
- iOS capture/review bridge.
- watchOS capture shell.
- Backend auth, upload, sync, transcription, workflow, and operations clients behind explicit gates.

## Risks
- Deployed provider orchestration and App Store signing need direct proof before release claims.

## Validation
- Verify with Swift tests, mock backend self-test, production verifier, app launch smoke, and log review.

artifacts.title: UX Flow: IdeaForge
artifacts.markdown:
# UX Flow: IdeaForge

## User Journey
- Capture an idea from Watch, iPhone, or Mac.
- Review transcript, questions, assumptions, validation, workflow runs, and artifacts.
- Export a build packet after review.

## Screens
- Inbox, project overview, transcript, questions, runs, artifacts, Codex handoff, account, and export review.

## States
- Empty, queued, recording, uploading, failed, offline, permission denied, review needed, and ready-for-build states.

## Edge Cases
- Interrupted upload, duplicate transfer, sync conflict, revoked permissions, long transcript, and partial workflow failure.

## Validation
- Exercise the running app after layout or workflow changes.

artifacts.title: Data Model: IdeaForge
artifacts.markdown:
# Data Model: IdeaForge

## Entities
- IdeaProject, Recording, Transcript, Artifact, WorkflowRun, UploadJob, Question, Assumption, ValidationExperiment, CodexTask, BackendSession, and EntitlementSummary.

## Relationships
- Projects own recordings, transcript segments, questions, artifacts, assumptions, validation experiments, workflow runs, and Codex tasks.

## Storage
- Local JSON workspace state, encrypted local audio objects, Keychain secrets, scoped backend objects, and reviewable export files.

## Retention Rules
- Never delete local audio before confirmed safe state; redact transcript, audio path, token, and credential details from logs.

## Risks
- Retention and deletion behavior needs physical-device and deployed-backend proof.

artifacts.title: API Design: IdeaForge
artifacts.markdown:
# API Design: IdeaForge

## Endpoints
- Account provisioning, auth session validation, signed recording upload, workspace sync, transcription job, workflow execution, account usage, restore drill, and backup manifest.

## Payloads
- Requests include stable project IDs, upload-job idempotency keys, content length, SHA-256 digests, schema names, review status, and workspace scope.

## Auth Scope
- Use workspace-bound sessions, Keychain-backed credentials, explicit backend configuration, privacy-mode gates, and entitlement checks.

## Failure Modes
- Fail closed on digest mismatch, idempotency conflict, missing configuration, revoked credentials, schema mismatch, entitlement exhaustion, and unsafe deletion state.

## Validation
- Mock backend and provider contract tests must reject malformed responses.

artifacts.title: Roadmap: IdeaForge
artifacts.markdown:
# Roadmap: IdeaForge

## Scope
- Productionize capture, local persistence, workflow artifacts, backend seams, sync safety, export review, and release gates.

## Sequence
1. Keep the gap map and readiness docs current.
2. Complete schema-backed build packets.
3. Prove backend/provider orchestration through mock and deployed contracts.
4. Run physical-device, performance, and App Store signing checks.

## Risks
- Shipping claims are blocked until deployed backend, device, and signing evidence exist.

## Next Steps
- Run production verifier and collect runtime screenshots for each UI-facing slice.

artifacts.title: Issue Bundle: IdeaForge
artifacts.markdown:
# Issue Bundle: IdeaForge

## Issues
- Build capture reliability, transcription orchestration, workflow execution, sync conflict recovery, export handoff, billing, and release-readiness slices.

## Labels
- macOS, iOS, watchOS, privacy, backend, workflow, verification, release, and App Store.

## Dependencies
- Capture and storage precede upload; transcript review precedes artifact export; schema validation precedes Codex handoff; entitlement proof precedes paid AI access.

## Acceptance Checks
- Run swift test, mock backend self-test, production verifier, macOS launch smoke, iOS UI smoke, release-signing check, and privacy-safe log review.

## Validation
- Each issue needs an observable command, screenshot, or log artifact.

artifacts.title: Codex Packet: IdeaForge
artifacts.markdown:
# Codex Packet: IdeaForge

## Repo Context
Implement inside /Users/example/dev/apps/IdeaForge using the existing SwiftPM, XcodeGen, SwiftUI, and IdeaForgeCore service patterns.

## Tasks
- Inspect current source, docs, and tests before editing.
- Implement the next production slice without weakening privacy gates.
- Keep external writes and Codex/GitHub execution behind explicit operator approval.

## Checks
- Run swift test.
- Run python3 script/mock_backend.py --self-test.
- Run ./script/verify_production.sh and ./script/build_and_run.sh --verify.

## Approval Boundary
No public writes, store submission, real credentials, or unreviewed external execution without explicit approval.

## Risks
- Codex task execution can mutate state if approval boundaries drift.

artifacts.title: Launch Checklist: IdeaForge
artifacts.markdown:
# Launch Checklist: IdeaForge

## Release Gates
- Clean tests, production verifier, signing review, package validation, upload readiness, privacy review, and fail-closed external blockers.

## App Store Assets
- App icon, screenshots, metadata, privacy nutrition labels, support URL, review notes, subscription products, and account-deletion URL.

## Privacy Checks
- Verify local-first storage, Keychain secrets, redacted logs, explicit backend opt-in, no raw transcript/audio/path/token leakage, and retention behavior.

## Monitoring Checks
- Confirm telemetry categories, restore drill, backup manifest, upload retry visibility, sync health, crash review, and rollback instructions.

## Next Steps
- Mark physical-device, deployed-backend, and App Store account checks blocked until direct evidence exists.
